channel.title: Reverb — New Bass Guitars
channel.description: Recently listed bass guitars on Reverb

item.title: Squier Affinity Jazz Bass VI 2024 - Metallic Black — $344.45
item.description:
$344.45 · Excellent · Reidys Home of Music

This second hand  bass guitar is in great condition

The Squier Affinity Jazz Bass VI is a fun, affordable, extended‑range instrument that blends bass and guitar roles. It offers a playable, inspiring entry point into the world of baritone/extended‑range basses for players who want something different without spending big money.

Squier’s Affinity Jazz Bass VI is essentially a six‑string, short‑scale bass/guitar hybrid, tuned E–E like a standard guitar but an octave lower.

  • Thin contoured offset body
  • Graphite-reinforced slim “C”-shaped neck
  • Squier single-coil pickups
  • Lightweight sealed-gear tuning machines
  • 6-saddle bass bridge, 17mm string spacing

item.title: Sterling Sting ray sting ray ray 35 2024-present - Spalted maple — $765
item.description:
$765 · Excellent · Jeff's Gear Outlet

Ray 35 burned top has been professionally setup new roundhouse strings. Has one chip in back of body just in black paint around Edge not wood by the wiring harness cover otherwise excellent hardly played, we are just not a good match. But it's so damn sexy. The stickers are held on under pick guard not actually stuck on!!

item.title: Markbass MB GV5 GOG CR MP FG - Red Pass — $898.69
item.description:
$898.69 · Brand New · Musical Pontevedra

Bajo eléctrico de 5 cuerdas Markbass MB GV 5 GOG en elegante acabado Red Pass. Pertenece a la serie GV de Markbass, que ofrece instrumentos de gran calidad a un precio muy competitivo. Cuerpo J-Style de aliso, mástil de arce, diapasón de arce con 20 trastes y block inlays, escala de 34" y separación de cuerdas de 18 mm. Electrónica pasiva con pastillas MB Noiseless JJ-Style para un sonido nítido y libre de zumbidos. Hardware cromado y funda Markbass incluida.

El Markbass MB GV 5 GOG lleva el inconfundible carácter de un bajo de configuración J-Style al formato de 5 cuerdas, añadiendo el registro grave extendido del Si bajo (B) sin renunciar a la fiabilidad y el cuidado de fabricación de Markbass. Pertenece a la serie GV, la gama con la que la marca italiana demuestra su capacidad para producir instrumentos de alta calidad a un precio sorprendentemente accesible, fruto de una cuidadosa selección de materiales y componentes. Su acabado Red Pass le aporta un aspecto elegante y con carácter sobre el escenario.

Construido con cuerpo J-Style de aliso (alder) y unión atornillada, combina un mástil de arce con un diapasón también de arce —que potencia un ataque brillante y definido—, 20 trastes y block inlays. La escala de 34" mantiene una tensión equilibrada incluso en la quinta cuerda grave, mientras que la cejilla de 45 mm y la separación de cuerdas de 18 mm ofrecen un tacto cómodo y bien repartido, idóneo tanto para el fingerstyle como para el slap y los ritmos más exigentes en cinco cuerdas.

En el plano sonoro, monta un juego de pastillas MB Noiseless JJ-Style, diseñadas para ofrecer el característico timbre J —nítido, articulado y con presencia— pero eliminando el típico zumbido (hum) de las bobinas simples, lo que las hace especialmente apropiadas para grabación y para escenarios con mucha electrónica alrededor. La electrónica es pasiva, con un esquema sencillo y musical de dos potenciómetros de volumen (uno por pastilla) y un control de tono pasivo, que permite mezclar ambas pastillas con precisión y obtener un sonido natural, orgánico y muy versátil.

El instrumento se completa con hardware cromado, cuerdas Markbass, la pala Mark Double Shape con acabado a juego y la funda Markbass incluida para proteger tu nuevo bajo.

¿Para quién es este bajo? Una opción excelente para bajistas que necesitan el registro grave extendido de las 5 cuerdas con el sonido clásico J sin zumbidos, fiable y de calidad, con la garantía de Markbass y a un precio muy competitivo. Ideal para funk, pop, rock moderno, gospel, jazz y estilos donde se valora la claridad, la definición y los graves profundos.

item.title: Sire Marcus Miler V5 5 String Bass Jazz bass roasted maple — $529
item.description:
$529 · Very Good · DB Music

Sire Marcus Miller V5. In great shape. No noticeable wear. No big dings or blemishes. Please review all pictures. The electronics work as they should. The action is currently low and it plays well to me but it may need set up to your personal preference. It comes shipped ready to play. Make an offer today. 

All orders include Reverb shipping protection and professional packaging. 

PLEASE REVIEW ALL PICTURES. PLEASE DO NOT MAKE AN OFFER UNLESS YOU ARE READY TO PURCHASE. Thank you. 

We do our best to show detailed pictures and provide accurate descriptions of all our items. We sell used items; there for most items will show typical wear from use. All items are sold as is. Guitars may need set up to your personal preference. We believe in doing fair business and will always provide the most accurate information we can on our items. Please feel free to make a reasonable offer! We expect all accepted offers to be paid within 24 hours. We usually ship next day. Items ordered on weekends will be shipped by next business day. Reverbs policy is three days. All items can be viewed/purchased at DB Music in Zephyrhills, Florida

item.title: Markbass MB5 JB - Amarillo — $448.78
item.description:
$448.78 · Brand New · Musical Pontevedra

Bajo eléctrico de 5 cuerdas Markbass MB 5 Yellow JB con la inconfundible configuración Jazz (J-Style). Cuerpo de álamo, mástil de arce y diapasón de laurel con 20 trastes, escala de 34". Dos pastillas de bobina simple, dos controles de volumen y uno de tono en electrónica pasiva, para ese sonido J nítido, articulado y versátil. Calidad Markbass a un precio muy competitivo. Incluye funda y correa.

El Markbass MB 5 Yellow JB lleva el inconfundible carácter del bajo Jazz (J-Style) al formato de 5 cuerdas, añadiendo el registro grave extendido del Si bajo (B) sin renunciar al timbre nítido, brillante y versátil que ha hecho de esta configuración una de las más populares de todos los tiempos. Markbass lo reinterpreta con su característico cuidado de fabricación y una excelente relación calidad-precio, vestido con un vistoso acabado Yellow que destaca sobre el escenario.

Construido con cuerpo de álamo estilo J y unión atornillada, combina un mástil de arce con un diapasón de laurel y 20 trastes, sobre una escala de 34" que mantiene una tensión equilibrada incluso en la quinta cuerda grave. El resultado es un instrumento cómodo y resolutivo, idóneo tanto para el fingerstyle como para el slap y los ritmos más exigentes en cinco cuerdas.

En el plano sonoro, monta dos pastillas de bobina simple en configuración J, el corazón del sonido Jazz Bass: un timbre claro, articulado y con presencia, capaz de cubrir desde graves profundos y redondos (favoreciendo la pastilla del mástil) hasta tonos más nasales y cortantes (con la pastilla del puente). La electrónica pasiva, con dos controles de volumen (uno por pastilla) y un control de tono, permite mezclar ambas pastillas con precisión y obtener una enorme variedad de matices con una respuesta natural y directa, ideal para funk, pop, rock, jazz y prácticamente cualquier estilo.

Como detalle de valor añadido, el instrumento se entrega con funda y correa incluidas, listo para tocar desde el primer momento.

¿Para quién es este bajo? Una elección perfecta para bajistas que necesitan el registro grave extendido de las 5 cuerdas con el sonido clásico y versátil del Jazz Bass, fiable y de buena calidad, con la garantía de Markbass y a un precio muy ajustado.

item.title: Schecter Hellraiser Extreme-5 Electric Bass Crimson Red Burst Satin B-Stock 1027 — $1,079.20
item.description:
$1,079.20 · B-Stock · LV Guitars

Schecter Hellraiser Extreme-5 Electric Bass Crimson Red Burst Satin B-Stock

The Hellraiser Extreme-5 delivers a slim, fast neck profile that cuts fatigue during extended playing sessions, while the neck-thru construction with Ultra Access ensures effortless reach to the upper frets. This B-stock unit features cosmetic flaws in the headstock area but maintains full structural and tonal integrity. The extended 35" scale and 24 X-Jumbo frets provide the articulation and sustain demanded by modern bass players.

Playability & Construction

The neck-thru design with mahogany body and quilted maple top yields solid sustain and resonance, while the Maple/Walnut multi-ply neck with ebony fretboard resists wear and delivers consistent response. The Thin 'C' neck shape (0.787" at the 1st fret, 0.866" at the 12th fret) paired with a 16" fretboard radius and 1.771" nut width accommodates both aggressive finger technique and smooth thumb positioning across the five strings.

Electronics & Tone

Dual EMG 40TW pickups feed a dedicated active 3-band EQ circuit with push-pull volume controls, delivering everything from tight lows to articulate highs with minimal noise. The 18-volt battery compartment with screw plate ensures stable power delivery and easy access for maintenance.

Key Features
  • Body Material: Mahogany with Quilted Maple top
  • Neck-Thru construction with Ultra Access
  • Maple/Walnut multi-ply neck with Thin 'C' profile
  • Ebony fretboard with MOP Gothic Cross inlays
  • 24 X-Jumbo frets on 35" (889mm) scale
  • Dual EMG 40TW pickups with active 3-band EQ
  • Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut
  • Schecter Custom Bass String Thru bridge
  • Grover tuners with Black Chrome hardware
  • MOP binding with BLK/WHT/BLK multi-ply
  • 16" fretboard radius with 2-way adjustable truss rod
  • 18-volt battery compartment with screw plate

This Hellraiser Extreme-5 combines high-end hardware and active electronics in a platform built for speed and tonal range, with the B-stock discount reflecting cosmetic finish rather than any compromise in playability or sound.

This specific B-Stock unit features minor cosmetic imperfections typical of returned or display inventory. These marks are purely superficial and have absolutely zero impact on the playability, tone, or structural integrity of the bass. A premium Schecter Hellraiser Extreme-5 active bass is available at significant B-Stock savings with complete manufacturer warranty coverage.

item.title: 【new】Sadowsky / MX20 UV5 CPM/R 4.18kg #SMX A 000312-26【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,524
item.description:
$1,524 · Excellent · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sadowsky is a high-end New York brand with top-tier musicians such as Marcus Miller and Will Lee as clients. (Titles omitted)

Previously, the Metroline Express was manufactured in Japan, but as Warwick took over the distribution of Sadowsky products, manufacturing shifted to a factory in China.

While keeping the selling price down, high quality is maintained, making it a very unique product in its price range.

This is the MX20 UV5, the first PB-type guitar in the MX series.

The body is made of lightweight solid Okoume, the neck is made of Roasted Maple, and the fingerboard is made of Morado (Pau Ferro).

This is a standard combination in the MX series.

The construction has been thoroughly considered for excellent body balance and sound projection, resulting in a very refined balance that is comfortable to hold.

The back of the neck has a satin finish, providing a smooth and pleasant feel.

The pickup is a "Sadowsky passive P-Style pickup."

It has the powerful, forceful projection characteristic of PB-type basses, yet delivers a clear sound without muddy bass tones.

The preamp features the familiar 2-band EQ.

You can significantly boost the bass and treble frequencies with the touch of a button.

The circuit board of this preamp is almost identical to that of the top-of-the-line models priced around 1 million yen.

Copper foil shielding is also applied, resulting in surprisingly low noise.

These points are extremely significant advantages when choosing an MX series bass.

Even in this price range, Roger Sadowsky's uncompromising commitment to instrument making is evident.

While offering the ultra-smooth playability characteristic of Sadowsky, the sound is unmistakably that of a P-bass.

It outputs a modern tone that beautifully blends a robust punch with sweet, airy, and clear treble tones.

Based on the traditional PB design, this guitar incorporates modern elements, embodying Roger Sadowsky's philosophy.

SPECS

Scale Length: 34" (864 mm) Long Scale
Body Shape: P-Style body
Body Wood: Okoume, Solidbody
Neck Wood: Roasted Maple (C-Shape, Bolt-On)
Fingerboard Wood: Morado
Inlays: White dot inlays
Fingerboard Radius: 12"
Nut: Sadowsky Just-A-Nut III
Nut Width: 1.875" (47.5 mm)
Nut String Spacing: Nut string spacing (all strings): 1.54" / 39.1 mm
Neck width at 12th fret: 2.7" / 68.6 mm neck width at 12th fret
Neck width at last fret: 3.1" / 78.7 mm neck width at last fret
Neck thickness at 1st fret: 0.86" / 21.8 mm neck thickness at 1st fret
Neck thickness at 12th fret: 0.93" / 23.6 mm neck thickness at 12th fret
# Frets: 20 Nickel Silver (width: 0.094" (2.4 mm) / height: 0.051" (1.3 mm))
Neck Pickup: Sadowsky passive P-Style pickup
Bridge Pickup: None
Preamp: Active Sadowsky 2-way electronics (Bass and Treble boost)
Controls: Volume / Treble / Bass
Push/Pull Function: Push/pull function at Bass knob for preamp bypass
Machine Heads: Sadowsky Light Open Gear Machine Heads
Security Locks: Sadowsky S-Security Locks
Bridge: Sadowsky bridge with quick string release
Bridge Total String Spacing: 2.83" / 72mm
Hardware Color: Chrome
Strings: Sadowsky Silver Label, Stainless Steel, 045 - 130
Protector: Sadowsky Fretboard Protector
Tools: Hex key tools
Bag / Case: Sadowsky PortaBag
Made In: China

Accessories: Original gig bag, warranty card

Text: Kaigo Kawami

Product photos: Muragishi

Manufacturer's warranty included

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made in Japan)

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.
Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

item.title: Hagstrom Viking Bass 2020s - Cherry — $435
item.description:
$435 · Good · Bob's Bass Bunker

Rescued, refurbished and reconditioned Hagstrom Viking bass which had pickups, tailpiece, bridge, and pickguard removed, and six-way switch damaged. The Hagstrom Viking Bass is semi hollow with a 30.75 inch scale.
This semi acoustic bass guitar has classic styling. It comes with some very modern touches though. Play-ability wise maple set neck is slim and very comfortable. The Hagstrom Viking Bass in Wild Cherry Transparent includes an H-Expander Truss Rod, which functions perfectly. This adds stability to the neck. Important given its slender proportions. Resinator wood is a composite used for the fretboard. It delivers tones similar to that of ebony. It is more consistent though.  Body and neck are in very good condition, with a few superficial nicks and scratches that do not affect playability. Tuning machines are original, and all work perfectly. Frets leveled, dressed and polished. The fretboard has been conditioned with oil. The original missing Tune-O-Matic Bridge has been replaced with a new one, and the tailpiece with a Hoffner tailpiece. Pickups replaced with high-output Hoffner-style humbuckers by Artec (MVBA4-C), which sound great and still have protective plastic on rings. Six-way switch replaced with mechanical rotary six-way switch which allows split-humbucker options as follows: 1)neck humbucker 2)bridge humbucker 3)both humbuckers 4)neck single coil (north) 5)bridge single coil (south) 6)neck & bridge outer single coils. These give a wonderful range of tones to work with. Each pickup has original volume and tone controls. No pickguard. The string spacing has been slightly adjusted to bring strings over pickups and more like Fender spacing, rather than the wide Hagstrom spacing. This allows for much better pickup efficiency and easy playability. Action adjusted to medium-low action and straight neck. The thin Hagstrom neck really makes this a pleasure to play. New set of GHS Brite Flats for short scale punch and character. New, these basses run about $1000. No case, but I will pack well-padded in an oversized box via UPS. Message me if you want more pictures or info.

This is a rare item that can only be obtained by those who purchase an Atelier Z bass guitar at Ikebe. Take this opportunity to find your dream Atelier Z!

Eligible products: Purchase an ATELIER Z bass guitar priced at 300,000 yen or more (including tax) and receive a free gift. *This is a limited-quantity gift, so please note that it will be discontinued once supplies run out.
*Some items, such as outlet items, sale items, reuse/second-hand items, and made-to-order items, may be excluded.
----------------------------------------

The M#245 is Atelier Z's flagship and ultra-classic model.

ATELIER Z is a long-established Japanese brand that enjoys widespread support from both professional and amateur players across a variety of genres. This particular model is the M#245, the company's flagship model widely known as a high-quality, domestically produced active JB bass. Furthermore, it boasts a backbone based on the intentions of Tomohito Aoki, and is highly regarded as a model favored by musicians.

While based on the 70s JB type, this bass incorporates a body shape with an original design that takes into consideration the prevention of the instrument tipping over, the installation of the unique low-action setting "Z Tuning System" that provides a pleasant buzz, the powerful active sound of the Bartolini XTCT (which also has a mid-cut control in the cavity), the original bridge with a substantial mass that is a characteristic of this brand, and a thick clear pickguard and pickup fence specification that allows for smooth control of thumbing and pulling when slapping, which is a specialty of this brand. The specifications and design are packed with attention to detail from a bassist's perspective. Furthermore, this instrument is made with the latest specification with an added master tone, allowing for fine nuances even when used in passive mode.

This bass boasts a dynamic and powerful bass thanks to its ash body, a pleasantly tight sound image, and a fast attack response thanks to its coated one-piece maple neck. You can enjoy the dynamic and clear sound of a 70's JB bass with a supple touch, making it truly a player's gear. Furthermore, the rich vibration produced by the full-size body and the pleasant, rustic tone resulting from the open resonance of the traditional neck construction without graphite support are also key features and appealing aspects of this model.

Furthermore, this model has been redesigned from the previous [2 Volume] configuration to a more smoothly controllable [Master Volume, Balancer] configuration, and the controls have been changed accordingly to "Master Volume/Master Tone (Stack), Balancer, Treble, Bass (Pull/Passive)". In addition to its distinctive sound that is instantly recognizable, the sense of security that comes from our workshop's carefully managed product quality control is one of the reasons why it has captivated so many bassists. Take this opportunity to get your hands on this authentic model that will make its presence felt on stage and in the recording studio.

*Some items, such as outlet items, sale items, reused/second-hand items, and made-to-order items, may be excluded.
----------------------------------------

This is a color-ordered version of the upgraded 24-fret Beta 5-string model.

ATELIER Z Guitar Works enjoys the deep trust of professional bassists of all musical styles. Among the brand's extensive lineup of basses, characterized by a clear sound orientation and a variety of specifications to meet the needs of players, this particular model is the "Beta," which features a 24-fret design and a deep cutaway on the treble side for excellent playability in the higher positions. It is equipped with an ash body, a celluloid-wrapped maple neck, and an original bridge. While retaining the tight and powerful bass tones that are characteristic of the brand's flagship model "M (Magnitude)," this model also features a sharp body shape, adding a crisp and well-defined nuance to its sound.

One of the great things about ATELIER Z basses is that they deliver a solid, resonant acoustic sound even with a low string action. Not only is the sound crisp when slapping, but you also get a well-defined bottom end when fingerpicking or using a pick. The thick pickguard and the exquisite Z-tuning system create a tight string spacing, allowing for supple responsiveness to fretting and picking nuances, contributing to stable playing and highlighting its excellence as a tool to support the player's expression. You can fully enjoy the slap sound that has become synonymous with this brand. Furthermore, even when boosting the sound on the bass itself during slapping, there's no loss of clarity or fragmentation of the sound image; instead, it significantly enhances the bass's presence. In addition to the brand's signature crisp sound, the 24-fret design offers nimble playability and ease of handling technical phrases, which are key to this model's popularity. It's also recommended for those who want to play various genres with a single bass.

The Beta series, which had been in the lineup for a long time, underwent a renewal in March 2025, and along with that, the models and specifications were also refreshed. The string spacing is now 19mm as standard, and the pickups are the company's original "JZ-5" pickups, which employ a slant pole piece/split coil structure, achieving a noiseless and powerful sound. Furthermore, it is equipped with the newly developed "AZ-3B" active preamp. Each of the 3 bands can be boosted/cut, covering a wide range of sounds. In addition, a heel cut at the joint and a removable finger ramp have been included, actively incorporating features for technically-oriented players. Be sure to get your hands on one of the two flagship models, which has been further enhanced in every aspect and has become even more powerful.

item.title: Tagima 5-String Bass Guitar with Sunburst Finish — $709.99
item.description:
$709.99 · Brand New · Mojo's Music

Tagima Predator 5 SB Multi-Scale 5-String Active Bass – Sunburst
Unleash the Predator. Command the Stage.

Ready to dominate the low end? The Tagima Predator 5 SB is a modern, multi-scale powerhouse engineered for bassists who demand flawless modern performance without sacrificing classic tonal soul. From gospel and jazz to heavy rock and fusion, this 5-string beast provides the surgical precision and floor-shaking power you need to hunt down the perfect groove.

Once it’s in your hands, you’re the hunter—not the hunted.

Key Features You'll Love:

  • Multi-Scale Perfection (34" to 35.5"): Ergonomic fan frets provide optimal, balanced string tension across all five strings. Say goodbye to a floppy low-B; experience crisp, tight articulation and effortless playability from the lowest growls to the highest solos.

  • Premium Tonewood Combination: Built with a resonant Okoume body capped with a striking Curly Maple top in a gorgeous Sunburst finish. Paired with a bulletproof Roasted Maple neck and a Bolivian Rosewood fingerboard for unmatched sustain, stability, and premium feel.

  • Surgical Sonic Control: Equipped with a versatile P-5 Neck and J-5 Bridge pickup configuration. Driven by the TBEQ-3P active preamp, you get total command over your mix with independent controls for Volume, Balance, Treble, Bass, and Mid/Mid Frequency.

  • Pro-Grade Hardware: Individual monorail bridge pieces isolate each string's vibration to minimize cross-talk and maximize clarity, while a precision bone nut ensures rock-solid tuning stability.

Specifications:

Component

Material / Feature

Body

Okoume with Curly Maple Top

Finish

Sunburst (SB)

Neck

Roasted Maple

Fingerboard

Bolivian Rosewood

Frets

Medium Nickel Multi-Scale (34" - 35.5")

Nut

Bone (45 mm)

Bridge

Monorail

Pickups

P-5 (Neck) / J-5 (Bridge)

Electronics

Active TBEQ-3P Preamp (Volume, Balance, Treble, Mid/Freq, Bass)

item.title: 【new】Sadowsky / MX20 UV5 TBT/R 3.78kg #SMX A 000280-26【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,524
item.description:
$1,524 · Excellent · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sadowsky is a high-end New York brand with top-tier musicians such as Marcus Miller and Will Lee as clients. (Titles omitted)

Previously, the Metroline Express was manufactured in Japan, but as Warwick took over the distribution of Sadowsky products, manufacturing shifted to a factory in China.

While keeping the selling price down, high quality is maintained, making it a very unique product in its price range.

This is the MX20 UV5, the first PB-type guitar in the MX series.

The body is made of lightweight solid Okoume, the neck is made of Roasted Maple, and the fingerboard is made of Morado (Pau Ferro).

This is a standard combination in the MX series.

The construction has been thoroughly considered for excellent body balance and sound projection, resulting in a very refined balance that is comfortable to hold.

The back of the neck has a satin finish, providing a smooth and pleasant feel.

The pickup is a "Sadowsky passive P-Style pickup."

It has the powerful, forceful projection characteristic of PB-type basses, yet delivers a clear sound without muddy bass tones.

The preamp features the familiar 2-band EQ.

You can significantly boost the bass and treble frequencies with the touch of a button.

The circuit board of this preamp is almost identical to that of the top-of-the-line models priced around 1 million yen.

Copper foil shielding is also applied, resulting in surprisingly low noise.

These points are extremely significant advantages when choosing an MX series bass.

Even in this price range, Roger Sadowsky's uncompromising commitment to instrument making is evident.

While offering the ultra-smooth playability characteristic of Sadowsky, the sound is unmistakably that of a P-bass.

It outputs a modern tone that beautifully blends a robust punch with sweet, airy, and clear treble tones.

Based on the traditional PB design, this guitar incorporates modern elements, embodying Roger Sadowsky's philosophy.

SPECS

Scale Length: 34" (864 mm) Long Scale
Body Shape: P-Style body
Body Wood: Okoume, Solidbody
Neck Wood: Roasted Maple (C-Shape, Bolt-On)
Fingerboard Wood: Morado
Inlays: White dot inlays
Fingerboard Radius: 12"
Nut: Sadowsky Just-A-Nut III
Nut Width: 1.875" (47.5 mm)
Nut String Spacing: Nut string spacing (all strings): 1.54" / 39.1 mm
Neck width at 12th fret: 2.7" / 68.6 mm neck width at 12th fret
Neck width at last fret: 3.1" / 78.7 mm neck width at last fret
Neck thickness at 1st fret: 0.86" / 21.8 mm neck thickness at 1st fret
Neck thickness at 12th fret: 0.93" / 23.6 mm neck thickness at 12th fret
# Frets: 20 Nickel Silver (width: 0.094" (2.4 mm) / height: 0.051" (1.3 mm))
Neck Pickup: Sadowsky passive P-Style pickup
Bridge Pickup: None
Preamp: Active Sadowsky 2-way electronics (Bass and Treble boost)
Controls: Volume / Treble / Bass
Push/Pull Function: Push/pull function at Bass knob for preamp bypass
Machine Heads: Sadowsky Light Open Gear Machine Heads
Security Locks: Sadowsky S-Security Locks
Bridge: Sadowsky bridge with quick string release
Bridge Total String Spacing: 2.83" / 72mm
Hardware Color: Chrome
Strings: Sadowsky Silver Label, Stainless Steel, 045 - 130
Protector: Sadowsky Fretboard Protector
Tools: Hex key tools
Bag / Case: Sadowsky PortaBag
Made In: China

Accessories: Original gig bag, warranty card

Text: Kaigo Kawami

Product photos: Muragishi

Manufacturer's warranty included

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made in Japan)

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.
Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

item.title: Ibanez Ibanez SR1500 Soundgear Bass 1991 - Natural — $1,083.12
item.description:
$1,083.12 · Very Good · WOLF GUITARS AUSTRALIA

One of the finest early Soundgear basses ever produced.

This 1991 Ibanez SR1500 was built by the renowned FujiGen factory in Japan during what many players consider the golden era of Japanese Ibanez production. Combining exceptional build quality, premium woods and a remarkably slim, fast-playing neck, the SR1500 remains one of the standout models from the original Soundgear series.

Finished in a beautiful Natural finish over an ash body, this bass features the original IBZ/USA C2 Precision/Jazz pickup combination, active electronics and a comfortable contoured body that has made the Soundgear series a favourite with players for more than three decades.

This bass is in very good vintage condition for its age. There are the expected cosmetic signs of a well-played instrument including light marks, minor finish wear, some tarnishing to the bridge and hardware and small bumps around the body and headstock. None of these affect the playability or structural integrity of the instrument.

The neck is straight, the electronics function correctly and the bass plays beautifully.

Specifications

  • 1991 Ibanez SR1500
  • Made in Japan (FujiGen)
  • Serial Number: F139569
  • Ash body
  • Natural finish
  • Multi-piece neck
  • Rosewood fingerboard
  • 24 frets
  • 34" scale
  • Original IBZ/USA C2 P/J pickups
  • Active electronics
  • Original bridge and tuners

A fantastic opportunity to own one of the best Japanese-built Soundgear basses from the early 1990s.

item.title: Modulus FB5 Funk Unlimited Flea Bass 2006 Black — $5,750
item.description:
$5,750 · Excellent · Cecilia's Gear Outlet

2006 Black Modulus Flea Bass FB5 5 String Funk Unlimited Bass. This is an iconic electric bass guitar designed in collaboration with Red Hot Chili Peppers bassist Flea. This bass also has the exact pickup mod (Lane Poor) that Flea of RHCP used on many records. Introduced in 1997, it served as Flea's primary instrument during the band's peak creative era, spanning albums from Californication to Stadium Arcadium. Amazing sounding bass with that famous carbon fiber neck that stays so straight that the action on the neck can be super low without buzz. Purchased new in 2010. It is Mint. Bass was never gigged. Not a scratch on this bass Mint showroom condition. The bass is rare and the original Lane Poor pickup that flea used is hard to find super rare as well. Comes with flawless case and original Bartolinni pickup as well. A steal at this price considering it's a very hard to find in this condition with a very rare pickup as well. I will also include a strap, modulus original brochure and modulus hat! Because Modulus models are high-end, USA-built instruments made in very small production numbers, they command premium prices. Vintage used "Flea"-labeled models routinely fetch anywhere from $3,000 to over $8,000 depending on the rarity and hardware configuration.

item.title: Fender Squier Classic Vibe Late '60s Jazz 4 String Electric Bass Guitar - Lake Placid Blue — $429
item.description:
$429 · Mint · Rayhill Audio

A tribute to the decade of its birth, the Classic Vibe Late '60s Jazz Bass® combines the luxurious playability that made it famous with the versatility and massive tone of its dual Fender-Designed alnico single-coil pickups. Player-friendly features include a slim, comfortable "C"-shaped neck profile with an easy-playing 9.5"-radius fingerboard and narrow-tall frets, as well as a vintage-style bridge with threaded saddles. This throwback Squier model also features 1960s-inspired headstock markings, rich-looking nickel-plated hardware and a slick vintage-tint gloss neck finish for an old-school vibe.

Features:

100% Designed by Fender

Inspired by 1960s-era Jazz Bass models

Fender-Designed alnico pickups

Vintage-tint gloss neck finish

Nickel-plated hardware

Case: sold separately

Weight: 9 lb 2 oz



Condition:

This instrument is in excellent, near mint condition. The techs found no issues and it has little to no play wear. It looks, plays and sounds great! It still has protective cellophane on the hardware. Please see pictures for overall condition of the guitar. The actual item for sale is shown in the photographs. 

More Info:

We are an authorized dealer for brand approved manufacturer refurbished guitars. These guitars are sent directly from the manufacturer to the authorized refurbishing center where they are fully inspected, set up and cleared by a certified technician. They are sent there for many reasons, including: returns, cosmetic flaws, minor repairs, surplus & discontinued or last years models. The guitars serial number may be partially altered and/or replaced and the back of the headstock is lightly stamped "used" by the authorized refurbishing center per manufacturer agreements. 


Shipping:

Guaranteed free, fast and safe UPS shipping to lower 48 states. International UPS expedited and USPS Priority shipping available to most countries. We ship our items the same or next business day. All items are professionally packed for safe transit. 


Returns:

We back every guitar we sell with a 14 day return policy. If you do not like the way it feels, plays, looks or sounds upon arrival, just let us know and you can return it. See our shop policy link for full details.

item.description:

A truly stunning piece featuring the natural grain of carefully selected wood!
The NB5BEM 432 BM-PRM Ash2p HH Stormy Ocean Blue 2025 has arrived!
Sugi is often described as a true work of art due to its extraordinary beauty. This instrument similarly showcases the beautiful, ethereal grain patterns formed as the wood grew naturally, while its precise and meticulous craftsmanship delivers superb sound and playability!

item.title: Ibanez SRC6 Baritone Guitar, Walnut, Flat, 2016 [I160906050] (07/01) — $669.42
item.description:
$669.42 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

A used Ibanez baritone guitar has just arrived!!

A baritone guitar plays an octave lower than a standard guitar.
This model has a 30-inch scale length, the same as the Bass VI.
It features two EMG humbucker pickups and a 3-band EQ.
You can fully enjoy aggressive tone shaping?something thatfs impossible with passive instruments.
The neck features the SRfs signature moderately thin profile, ensuring a comfortable playing experience.

There are signs of use, such as a cutaway on the body near the first string, a large dent near the end, and age-related scuffs around the pickups and on the back, but this is still a guitar thatfs perfectly playable. Weighing just 2.9 kg, itfs lightweight and exceptionally easy to handle!!

item.title: Gibson Thunderbird IV Vintage Sunburst, manufactured in 1999 [91129769] (07/01) — $2,182.06
item.description:
$2,182.06 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

A flagship Gibson bass model named after the sacred gDivine Birdh from Native American folklore.
The body is mahogany, the neck is a through-neck design made of mahogany and walnut, and the fingerboard is ebony.

There is some twisting visible in the neck, but at this time, it has virtually no impact on playability. The instrument will be sold as-is.
The original case is missing. Additionally, it appears the nut and pots have been replaced.
The frets have been leveled.
The headstock has numerous scratches, dents, and areas of peeling finish; there is a vertical crack in the finish on the front of the headstock, a horizontal crack in the finish extending from the back of the headstock to the back of the neck, and a crack in the finish near the junction between the fingerboard and the neck.
The body has numerous scratches and dents; there is paint peeling near the lower left corner of the bodyfs top; the top of the volume knob is missing; there are playing scratches on the pickguard and the surrounding body; and the metal parts show rust, tarnish, and pitting.
Neck Condition: Twisted
Truss rod: Has room to tighten or loosen
Fret Wear: 60?70% remaining
Year of Manufacture: 1999
Country of Manufacture: USA
Case: Soft case / Non-original
Accessories: None

item.description:
$733.79 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

Wefve just received a used model from the generation before the current one!

Manufactured at the Ensenada factory in Mexico, this Player Series combines a classic look with a slightly modern style, making it the standard for contemporary Fender guitars.

The Modern gCh shape neck feels comfortable without being too thick, and its satin finish ensures excellent playability.
The sound is powerful yet moderately full-bodied, with a touch of grit?giving it that quintessential gP-Bassh feel.

Both the neck condition and the truss rod action are currently in good shape.
There are two dents and cracks in the finish, each about 1.5 cm in size, on the back of the neck near the low frets. There are scuff marks under the bridge.
There are other signs of wear, such as picking marks, but it plays perfectly fine and is still a great guitar to use!!

This model is highly recommended whether as your first bass or as a backup.
Please take this opportunity to try it out!

item.title: KRAMER DMZ-5000 Fretless [08111] (07/01) — $2,034.01
item.description:
$2,034.01 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

This model was manufactured around 1980. Its most distinctive feature is undoubtedly the aluminum neck. Itfs a very sturdy neck and does not have a truss rod. This is a fretless model.

It features a PJ pickup configuration, but an additional pickup has been installed in the bridge position, allowing for a wide variety of sounds. Controls include front volume and tone, and rear volume and tone. The large switch is the pickup selector; the mini-switches control the bridge pickupfs wiring modes?series, parallel, and tap (output from the neck side only). The other mini-switch is believed to be a direct switch.

Currently, the neck has a slight forward bow, and the body shows numerous marks that appear to be repairs for cracks and scratches. A SELVA gig bag is included.

This is a rare find, so please consider taking advantage of this opportunity!

item.title: FREEDOM CUSTOM GUITAR RESEARCH Retro JB-4st LH [32] (07/01) — $2,388.04
item.description:
$2,388.04 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

Wefve just received a used Freedom Left-Handed JB?a flagship model from Japanfs leading high-end guitar maker!

Freedom Custom Guitar Research is based in Tokyofs Shitamachi district.
Many of the artisans involved in each stage of production have spent years in the instrument-making industry, so itfs no exaggeration to call them Japanfs premier group of craftsmen.

This particular guitar wefve just received is a left-handed version of the classic f60s JB style.
While it boasts a traditional look, itfs also dotted with modern features such as a 21-fret fingerboard with an extended nut and a cavity treated with conductive material.

It produces a very appealing sound, characterized by a low center of gravity and a dense, full-bodied midrange.

The neck is currently in good condition, but the truss rod has about a quarter turn left to tighten.
Overall, there are signs of use, including scratches, dents, peeling paint, and dents, as well as tarnish on the metal parts.
Additionally, there are screw holes on the body and pickguard for mounting a pickup fence, but the fence itself is not included.

To everyone looking for a left-handed model! Please give this a try!!
